children.heading: Historic District
children.label: 1 Historic District
children.text: The term “Historic District” means the Ste. Genevieve Historic District National Historic Landmark, as generally depicted on the Map.

children.heading: Historical Park
children.label: 2 Historical Park
children.text: The term “Historical Park” means the Ste. Genevieve National Historical Park established by subsection (b).

children.heading: Map
children.label: 3 Map
children.text: The term “Map” means the map entitled “Ste. Genevieve National Historical Park Proposed Boundary Addition”, numbered 571/177,464, and dated September 2021.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Special resource study
children.label: 5 Special resource study
children.text: The term “special resource study” means the study entitled “Ste. Genevieve Final Special Resources Study and Environmental Assessment, Missouri” and dated May 2016.

children.heading: State
children.label: 6 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Missouri.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), there is established the Ste. Genevieve National Historical Park in the State as a unit of the National Park System to preserve, protect, and interpret for the benefit of present and future generations the themes of French settlement, vernacular architecture, and community form and farming on the frontier associated with Ste. Genevieve.

children.label: A
children.text: sufficient land has been acquired for the Historical Park to constitute a manageable unit; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary has entered into a written agreement providing that land owned by the State, the City of Ste. Genevieve, or other entity within the Historic District shall be managed consistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Boundaries
children.label: c Boundaries
children.text: The boundaries of the Historical Park shall be the boundaries generally depicted on the Map.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: Authority to correct errors
children.label: 2 Authority to correct errors
children.text: The Secretary may correct any clerical or typographical errors in the Map.

children.label: A
children.text: donation;

children.label: B
children.text: purchase with donated or appropriated funds; or

children.label: C
children.text: exchange.

children.heading: Boundary revision
children.label: 2 Boundary revision
children.text: On the acquisition of any property within the Historic District under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall revise the boundary of the Historical Park to include the property.

children.label: A
children.text: may acquire, by donation, the land (including any improvements to the land) owned by the city of Ste. Genevieve, Missouri, and used as the visitor center for the Historical Park, as generally depicted on the Map as “Proposed Boundary Addition”; and

children.label: B
children.text: on acquisition of the land described in subparagraph (A), shall revise the boundary of the Historical Park to include the acquired land.

children.heading: Administrative facilities
children.label: 4 Administrative facilities
children.text: The Secretary may acquire, by purchase from a willing seller or by donation, not more than 20 acres of land in the vicinity of the Historical Park for administrative facilities for the Historical Park.

children.label: A
children.text: this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of title 54; and

children.label: ii
children.text: chapter 3201 of title 54.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date on which funds are made available to prepare a general management plan for the Historical Park, the Secretary shall prepare the general management plan in accordance with .section 100502 of title 54

children.heading: Submission to Congress
children.label: B Submission to Congress
children.text: On completion of the general management plan under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate the general management plan.

children.heading: Related sites
children.label: 3 Related sites
children.text: The Secretary may provide interpretative tours and educational programs at related historic and cultural sites within the Historic District associated with the purposes for which the Historical Park is established.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance and enter into cooperative agreements with the owner of a nationally significant property within the Historical Park or the Historic District, to identify, mark, interpret, improve, and restore the property.

children.label: A
children.text: conducting visitors through the property; and

children.label: B
children.text: interpreting the property for the public.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: A Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the total cost of any activity carried out under a cooperative agreement entered into under this subsection shall be not more than 50 percent.

children.heading: Form of non-Federal share
children.label: B Form of non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share of an activity carried out under a cooperative agreement entered into under this subsection may be in the form of donated property, goods, or services fairly valued.

children.heading: Changes or alterations
children.label: 4 Changes or alterations
children.text: No changes or alterations shall be made to any property or project covered by a cooperative agreement entered into under paragraph (1) unless the Secretary and the other party to the agreement agree to the changes or alterations.

children.label: A
children.text: the amounts made available to the property or project by the United States; or

children.label: B
children.text: the portion of the increased value of the property or project attributable to the amounts made available under this subsection, as determined at the time of the conversion, use, or disposal.

children.heading: Limited role of the Secretary
children.label: h Limited role of the Secretary
children.text: Nothing in this section authorizes the Secretary to assume overall financial responsibility for the operation, maintenance, or management of the Historic District.
